Mike said: “At the moment we’re trying to bring her back to Portsmouth – her spiritual home where she was based in the 1970s and 1980s.



“But first we need to lift her off the seabed and get her onto a slipway.

“Then we’d have to decontaminate the vessel because there is asbestos and various oils still on board.”

RECOMMENDED STORIES

If that proves impossible, they plan to salvage key historic components such as her sonar equipment.



However, if the funds cannot be raised, the historic ship could be scrapped, which Mike warns would be a devastating end for such a unique piece of Britain’s naval history.

News.title: Baffled cops release new clues in hunt to identify body of tattooed man found in council wheelie bin after ‘hit-and-run’
News.content:
BIN MYSTERY Baffled cops release new clues in hunt to identify body of tattooed man found in council wheelie bin after ‘hit-and-run’

NEW clues have been released by police as they try to identify the body found inside a wheelie bin.

Cops have released images today of a green bin in which the body of a man was found after it had been dragged into a park.





The grim discovering was reported on Friday 5pm, when officers scrambled to Cash’s Park off Daimler Road in Coventry.

It’s believed the man was hit by a vehicle before being moved to a muddy path in the park.

The police are trying to establish where the bin had been moved from alongside the local authority.

Alongside images of the bin, police have also released further information about the man to help identify him, and hopefully determine the cause of death.



The man is said to be aged between 40 and 50.

He has a tattoo of a cross with a snake wrapped around it on his back and the words “Little Stardust”, and a tattoo on his right arm saying ‘nan’ with a clover and the colours of the Irish flag, the police said.

Police are urging anyone with information, or dashcam or CCTV footage from the area, to reach out.

Detective Chief Inspector Phil Poole, leading the investigation, said: “We have a large team of detectives, forensic specialists and other staff working around the clock to establish what has happened to this man, who he is, and who is responsible.



“We’ve had several pieces of information come through to us as a direct result of our appeal, and we are following up those lines of enquiry.

“I really want to hear from anyone who recognises the descriptions of the tattoos on the man, as they are quite distinctive.

“I also really want to hear from anyone who has noticed unexplained damage on a vehicle, perhaps of a partner or relative, or anyone who’s noticed a sudden change in behaviour from someone they know.

“If you know or are responsible for what happened to this man, I would urge you to come forward now so that we can get the answers that he deserves.”

You can contact them via 101, quoting log 4148 of 13 March.

News.title: All the England school holiday dates for 2026 revealed – including May half-term
News.content:
IF you are looking forward to a break from the school run, or need to find a holiday club for your kids – here are all the English school holiday dates this year.

Students have returned to classrooms following their half term which started on Monday, 16 February, and ended on Friday, 20 February 2026.



The new term started again on Monday, 23 February, but some school holidays may differ from school to school as they account for extra days or staff training.



Schools will next be out for Easter break which takes place between 30 March 2026 and 10 April 2026.

And although once again, exact days may differ depending on where you live in the country, all schools will be closed Friday, 3 April, and Monday, 6 April, as they are bank holidays.

Term starts on Monday, 13 April, and the summer half term break is 25 May – 29 May 2026.

Summer break is from 23 July until 31 August, but some schools such as Bedfordshire break up earlier on 20 July, so please check your own area.



The dates reflect the days community and voluntary controlled schools are open to pupils. This provides the required 190 days.

Schools can choose when they take staff training days so please check with your school to confirm when these will take place.

Term dates may vary for all other types of schools (VA, Foundation or Academy), so please check the individual school website for details.

You can check the exact dates for your postcode here.

News.title: Iran’s injured new leader ‘flown to MOSCOW for leg surgery’ after Putin ‘personally offered help’ as death rumours swirl
News.content:
IRAN’s new supreme tyrant has reportedly been flown to Moscow for leg surgery “personally offered by Putin” after suffering serious injuries during airstrikes.

It comes as rumours swirl that Mojtaba Khamenei could be dead – the leader has not been seen since his appointment.





Kuwaiti newspaper Al-Jarida reported that the son of the slaughtered Ayatollah had been snuck out of Iran for a top secret surgery.

The paper said that the evacuation took place under the strictest secrecy, and that the new Iranian leader was flown to Moscow on a Russian military aircraft.

Khamenei then reportedly underwent a “successful” operation in one of Putin’s presidential palaces.

The report is unconfirmed, but Al-Jarida said its information came from a “high-ranking source close to the new Iranian Supreme Leader”.

Khamenei was injured in one of the first blasts of operation Epic Fury, the source claimed.

Follow The Sun’s liveblog for the latest updates on Iran

His injuries needed a well-equipped and specialist hospital, which was impossible as Iran continues to be blitzed by the US and Israel.



Putin reportedly proposed treating the new dictator in a call on Thursday with Khamenei transferred to Russia that evening.

It comes after The Sun reported that the new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei was put in charge despite being critically wounded in an air strike.

Sources told The Sun the 56-year-old son of assassinated Ayatollah Ali Khamenei lost at least one leg and has also suffered serious stomach or liver damage.

It was unclear whether he was injured on the same day his 86-year-old father died, but he is not directing the strategy currently sparking energy market turmoil.

In a bizarre attempt to cover up their stricken Ayatollah, Iranian state TV broadcast what it claims to be his first statement since coming into power.



The statement – read out by a news anchor – said Iran will not hesitate to “avenge the blood of Iranians” who have been killed as it vowed to continue attacks on Dubai and block off the vital Strait of Hormuz.

A Sun source in Iranian capital Tehran last week said the new Ayatollah was in intensive care at the Sina University Hospital.

The source – who asked not to be identified fearing for his life – managed to dodge Iran’s near total internet blackout to send messages to an exiled dissident based in London.



He is not a medic but knows members of the hospital’s trauma team who told him Mojtaba was in “very serious” condition under the care of Mohammad Reza Zafarghandi.

He said: “One or two of his legs have been cut off. His liver or stomach has also ruptured. He is apparently in a coma as well.”

Our source’s detailed revelations are impossible to verify under Iran’s current internet blackout but Mojtaba’s status among Iran’s thousands of war wounded has already been confirmed.

Iranian state TV now openly refers to the late ayatollah’s silent successor as “Jaanbaz of Ramadan” – wounded war veteran.



And the rogue nation’s continuing attacks on neighbouring states – which have sent oil and gas prices soaring – supports theories that Iran is now being run by a “Ghost Ayatollah.”

Iran has also been blasted for using AI to edit images of new supreme leader.

Trump did little to play down rumours that Khamenei was killed in an airstrike, claiming “Iran wants to make a deal”.

He said: “I don’t know if he’s even alive.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“So far, nobody’s been able to show him.”



Iran insisted its new supreme tyrant was alive and well, with the rogue nation’s foreign minister claiming Khamenei is “in good health” and “fully controlling the situation”.

News.title: Cops make arrests as thousands of pro-Iran protesters gather for Al-Quds ‘hate rally’ in central London despite ban
News.content:
THREE arrests have been made in central London amid a protest and counter-protest during the Al Quds Day “hate march”, cops say.

Police had been bracing for thousands of people to attend, with hundreds of officers seen in the area.





Scores of people began arriving from 1pm, many holding Palestine flags and banners, some reading “Free Palestine” and “No to Israeli occupation”.

One demonstrator held a sign saying “boom boom Tel Aviv”.



Chants of “from the river to the sea” and “Israel is a terror state” could be heard.

The Metropolitan Police said they were “aware of chanting made by a speaker at the Al Quds protest and will be investigating”.

Pictures of Iran‘s late leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei were also held up, some accompanied by the message “Choose the right side of history“.

Counter-protesters have assembled on separate sides of the River Thames for the Al Quds Day demonstration in central London, organised by Stop The Hate.



Iranian dissidents are expected to be among the attendees, the group said.



In a post on X, the Metropolitan Police said: “Protesters have assembled on both sides of the river. The Al Quds day protest on the Albert Embankment and the counter-protest in Millbank.

“Conditions are in place stating the protests must conclude by 1500hrs.”

Police vans were parked along the road and on nearby Lambeth Bridge.

A police boat was seen in the River Thames.

Scotland Yard is bracing for a “difficult public order” environment today, with at least 1,000 officers drafted in to manage crowds expected of around 12,000 people as conflict continues in Iran.



Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood granted the police’s bid for a month-long ban of the annual march organised by the Islamic Human Rights Commission (IHRC), making it the first time such a restriction on protest had been imposed since 2012.

However, people can still legally assemble and take part in a so-called “static protest”.



The IHRC said the demonstration will take place “in defiance of a Government ban on the march”.

The annual Al Quds Day demonstration in London had drawn criticism over apparent backing for the Iranian regime after its organisers expressed support for the country’s late leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

Anyone who marches or incites others to march will also face arrest, the Met said.

The Met said in December that protesters chanting “globalise the intifada” would now be arrested as the “context has changed” in the wake of the Bondi Beach terror attack in Australia.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Police patrols will also be deployed around places of worship, community venues and embassies on Sunday, Met Assistant Commissioner Ade Adelekan said.



Al Quds Day is named after the Arabic name for Jerusalem and is usually held on the last Friday of Ramadan.

News.title: UK ‘intensively’ looking at how to reopen Strait of Hormuz, says Ed Miliband after Donald Trump’s call to send warships
News.content:
BRITAIN is “intensively” weighing options to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz, Ed Miliband said today.

The Shadow Energy Secretary suggested minesweepers and minehunting drones could be sent to the Middle East to counter Iran’s choke hold on the vital global shipping route.





Asked on Sky News about the possibility of intervening, Miliband said: “We are talking to our allies.

“There’s different ways in which we can make maritime shipping possible.

“We are intensively looking with our allies at what can be done, because it’s so important that we get the strait reopened.”



The government said on Saturday it is discussing a “range of options” to secure the key oil route.

But Miliband said on Sunday with Laura Kuenssberg that he would not provide operational details that the government is considering.

It comes amid mounting fears Iran has begun laying mines in the narrow sea passage used by oil tankers.

The exact size of Tehran’s mine stockpile is unclear, but estimates suggest it could hold between 2,000 and 6,000 naval mines, including Iranian, Chinese and Russian-made variants.



Several oil tankers have come under fire while trying to pass through the strait since the conflict began.

Minehunting drones could be deployed from the Royal Navy’s Mine and Threat Exploitation Group, which is already stationed in the Middle East.

Miliband added the UK government is already in talks with allies, including the US, about how to secure the key shipping route.

He said he believes that the “best way is to de-escalate”.

On Saturday, US President Donald Trump urged the UK and other nations to send naval vessels to help secure shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.



His call came just a week after he said the US does not “need people that join wars after we’ve already won”, responding to reports Britain was weighing sending more warships to defend bases in the region.

Shadow Energy Secretary Claire Coutinho said Mr Trump’s request for help “should certainly be explored”.

She said: “Our principle is that we should take action where it is in the national interest and where it is protecting our military assets abroad.”

Speaking on Sunday with Laura Kuenssberg, she said that the UK should explore sending ships or drones to the Middle East if it is in the national interest.

Coutinho added that she believes it is within the national interest to have international shipping lanes open and to protect military assets abroad.



But Liberal Democrat leader Ed Davey says the UK should not send ships to help control the Strait of Hormuz.

He was rather scathing in his criticism of Trump, who he said has been very insulting of how the UK has offered help so far.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

He claimed Trump seemed to be “very reckless” in this “illegal, damaging war”.

Davey added that Britain should not be at the beck and call of an American president “who does not seem to know what he is doing”.

What is the Straight of Hormuz? The Strait of Hormuz is a key trade artery through which around 20% of the world’s oil passes. Since the beginning of the US-Israel war with Iran, numerous ships have been reported to have come under attack in the area. Iran’s new supreme leader said on Thursday that Iran should keep using the “lever of blocking” the Strait. About 3,000 or so ships usually sail through the corridor each month. In 2025, about 20 million barrels of oil passed through the Strait per day. It is deep enough for the world’s biggest crude oil tankers, and is used by the major oil and gas producers in the Middle East – and their customers.

News.title: Shocking moment thug launches savage attack on worker at 4-star hotel hitting him in the face with BIBLE as he’s jailed
News.content:
THIS is the shocking moment a man lashes out at staff at a hotel, viciously hitting a worker in the face with a bible.

Disgraceful footage shows William Poyning using the holy book to smash a man in the face before the scrap descends to the floor.





He launched the attack at a 4-star city centre hotel in Peterborough after he was asked by management to clear off the premises.

Staff urged him to leave the Bull Hotel, in Westgate on February 12 due to a separate incident.

After being escorted to an exit, the CCTV footage captures Poyning become violent as he launches his unholy attack.

Later in the video, the 45-year-old can be seen kicking the man while he is down on the ground.

The victim suffered a cut to his nose and bite marks to one of his fingers, according to cops.

Cambridgeshire Police were called to the scene, and Poyning, of no known address, was arrested.

After pleading guilty to assault causing actual bodily harm, he was sentenced to nine months in prison at Cambridge Magistrates’ Court on March 10.

PC Jess Broome said: “Poyning’s behaviour was aggressive and intimidating as he lashed out with the bible and attacked someone who was simply doing their job.



“We won’t tolerate attacks like this on staff working in hospitality or the nighttime economy.

“I’m pleased Poyning has now appeared in court to answer for his actions.”

News.title: Iran's new leader 'flown to Moscow' for surgery after being 'seriously injured'
News.content:
Iran's new leader 'flown to Moscow' for surgery after being 'seriously injured'

Mojtaba Khamenei is said to have been injured in the initial US-Israeli airstrikes that targeted Tehran on February 28 - and saw his father and former leader Ali Khamenei killed

View 3 Images Mojtaba Khamenei is reportedly injured (Image: KHAMENEI.IR/AFP via Getty Images)

Iran's new supreme leader has reportedly flown to Moscow for leg surgery which was "personally offered by Putin" after he was injured in airstrikes.

‌



Mojtaba Khamenei is said to have been injured in the initial US-Israeli strikes that targeted Tehran on February 28. It comes after Donald Trump said the new leader could be dead, having not been seen since his appointment. "I don't know if he's even alive. So far, nobody's been able to show him," Trump told NBC. "I'm hearing he's not alive, and if he is, he should do something very smart for his country, and that's surrender."

‌



Khamenei has now reportedly been flown out of Iran and underwent a "successful" operation at one of Putin's presidential palaces, according to Kuwaiti newspaper Al-Jarida.

‌



The outlet reports that the top-secret surgery saw Khamenei flown to Moscow on a Russian military aircraft. While the report has not yet been confirmed, Al-Jarida said it had received information from a "high-ranking source close to the new Iranian Supreme Leader".

View 3 Images Donald Trump has questioned if Mojtaba Khamenei is alive (Image: ISNA/AFP via Getty Images)

Due to the severity of his injuries, Khamenei was reportedly treated at a specialist hospital, made possible by Putin, who is said to have proposed treating him in a call on Thursday. The leader is believed to have been transferred to Russia that same evening.

‌



Iran's foreign minister Abbas Araghchi has meanwhile claimed Khamenei is "in full health and is fully managing the situation."

There has been much speculation over the leader's health in recent days. An Israeli assessment indicated he was wounded in the war's opening salvo, which killed other members of his family including his father Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

View 3 Images Iran's former leader Ali Khamenei was killed on February 28 (Image: AFP via Getty Images)

‌



On Friday, US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth said Mojtaba Khamenei was "wounded and likely disfigured". He added: "Iran has plenty of cameras and plenty of voice recorders. Why a written statement? I think you know why. His father: dead; he's scared, he's injured, he's on the run, and he lacks legitimacy."

After his father's death, Mojtaba Khamenei released a statement through Iran's state television news. He said he would avenge not just the killings of his father and other leaders by the US and Israel - but that of every Iranian who has died in this war.

"I assure everyone that we will not refrain from avenging the blood of your martyrs," he said. "The retaliation we have in mind is not limited only to the martyrdom of the great leader of the Revolution; rather, every member of the nation who is martyred by the enemy constitutes a separate case in the file of revenge."

Article continues below

He also promised to keep up attacks on Gulf Arab countries and use the effective closure of the strategic Strait of Hormuz as leverage against the US and Israel.

News.title: Urgent product recalls at Asda, Farmfoods and Argos over safety fears
News.content:
Urgent product recalls at Asda, Farmfoods and Argos over safety fears

The Food Standards Agency has issued urgent recalls for cheesecake and snacks over allergen risks, while one brand of air fryers also poses a fire hazard

View 4 Images Asda are urging customers to check a product they may have purchased (Image: Ceri Breeze via Getty Images)

Shoppers are being warned to check their kitchen appliances and cupboards following a series of product recalls from UK supermarkets.

‌



The Food Standards Agency (FSA) withdraws products for various reasons, including when items contain allergens not listed on the packaging or if a problem is identified with the product.

‌



When an item is recalled, for whatever reason, the FSA publishes details on what to do if you've purchased it, along with comprehensive information explaining why the product has been withdrawn from sale. The latest product recalls include desserts, snacks and air fryers.

‌



Customers are typically advised to take the product back to the shop and receive a full refund, with no receipt required. We've compiled the recent product recalls from supermarkets.

Asda

View 4 Images GU is advising all customers to return the cheesecake (Image: FSA)

Gü Indulgent Foods is recalling Gü Blonde Chocolate Cheesecake because it has been incorrectly packed with a cheesecake containing hazelnuts. Gü products are stocked in supermarkets throughout the UK, including Asda, reports the Liverpool Echo.

‌



- Product name: Gü Blonde Chocolate Cheesecake.

- Pack size: 2 x 82g.

- Batch code: 226044.

‌



- Use by date: March 12 2026.

The possible presence of hazelnuts poses a potential health risk for anyone with a hazelnut allergy. The Food Standards Agency (FSA) stated: "If you have bought the product and have an allergy to hazelnuts (nuts) do not eat it.

"Instead return it to the store from where it was bought for a full refund. No receipt required. For more information, please contact recall@gupuds.com" Gü Indulgent Foods has been instructed to reach out to the appropriate allergy support organisations, which will inform their members about the product recall.

‌



Farmfoods

View 4 Images Farmfood is recalling a product after incorrect ingredients labelling (Image: FSA)

Farmfoods is recalling its Ritz Cheese Sandwich product as it contains milk, soya and wheat (gluten), which are not highlighted on the label. This recall only affects products originating from Indonesia, and does not affect products originating from other countries.

- Product name: Ritz Cheese Sandwich.

‌



- Pack size: 91g.

- Batch code: All batch codes.

The Food Standards Agency stated: "If you have bought this product, and have an allergy or intolerance to any of the allergens listed, do not eat it. Instead, return it to the store from which you bought the product for a full refund. For further information contact the Farmfoods Customer Services on 0121 700 7160."

‌



Farmfoods commented: "We take consumer safety very seriously and sincerely apologise to our loyal customers for this situation and any inconvenience it may have caused."

The ingredients not labelled properly means the product poses a "health risk" to those with an allergy or intolerance to milk, an allergy to soya or an allergy or intolerance to wheat or gluten, or with coeliac disease.

Argos

View 4 Images Tower air fryers are being recalled from several retailers including Argos (Image: OPSS/Tower)

‌



Several retailers are pulling Tower air fryers from shelves. Affected models were produced between January 2022 and April 2023.

The items have been stocked by shops including Argos, B&M Bargains, DID, Poundland, Robert Dyas, Sainsbury's, Tesco, and the Tower Housewares website. The product poses a fire hazard as it can overheat and catch alight whilst in use. It fails to comply with the Electrical Equipment (Safety) Regulations 2016.

- T17023 Tower 2.2Ltr Manual Air Fryer.

‌



- T17061BLK Tower 4Ltr Manual Air Fryer.

- T17067 Tower 4Ltr Digital Air Fryer (Sainsbury's SKU: 143897281).

- T17087 Tower 2Ltr Compact Manual Air Fryer.

‌



- T17129L Tower Vortx 8L Air Fryer (Lidl).

- T17129 Tower Vortx 8L Air Fryer (Argos).

- Risk type: Fire.

Article continues below

The product has been recalled from customers. Anyone who owns an affected model should cease using it straight away and disconnect it from the mains.

For more details, including verifying whether your product is impacted and registering for a refund or replacement, visit the Tower Housewares webpage: https://www.tower-safety.co.uk.

News.title: Beatrice and Andrew's 'chilling' power play exposed as Princess shuns protocol
News.content:
Beatrice and Andrew's 'chilling' power play exposed as Princess shuns protocol

A body language expert noted that years before the Royal Family scandal with Andrew became public, that the former Duke and his daughter were 'deliberately' not following Royal protocol

View 3 Images Andrew and Beatrice didn't follow Royal protocol towards the late Queen

Even before the rising tensions between Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor and the Royal Family, he and his daughter Princess Beatrice deliberately put on a "power play" towards the others.

‌



During the late Queen Elizabeth's 93rd birthday celebrations, her granddaughter Beatrice was the only person who didn't give her a curtsy and instead simply just smiled.

‌



According to body language expert Judi James, Beatrice and Andrew's choice to not abide by the traditional etiquette shows signs the pair were already "deviating" from royal protocol six years before Andrew and Beatrice's relationship with the Royal Family faced scrutiny over their ties to paedophile Jeffrey Epstein.

‌



View 3 Images The expert suggested Andrew and Beatrice were displaying a 'power play' (Image: Getty Images)

Judi claims that Beatrice, 37, and Andrew stood by the entrance of the chapel, and positioned themselves away from the rest of the royals in a "deliberate" fashion so that the former prince could "gloat about his legendary role as the late Queen's favourite son".

When the Queen arrived at Windsor's St George's Chapel in April 2019, she was greeted by a number of royals including Princess Kate, Zara Tindall and Sophie, Duchess of Edinburgh, who all curtseyed.

‌



The expert told the Daily Mail: "Beatrice clearly shuns the act of respectfully greeting the other royal women so studiously and emphatically and stands upright and stares as her grandmother passes and walks into the church. It gives the impression that Andrew is performing some status-leap frogging to define his own 'special role' in the family with a spot of non-verbal power play.

View 3 Images Duchess of Cambridge curtsying to Queen Elizabeth II (Image: Getty Images)

"He makes a bee-line for the spot nearest the door so he and Beatrice can dart in right behind the Queen, leaving Princess Anne lingering to one side and his brother Edward right at the back of the royal line." She further noted that both Beatrice and her father's greetings were "deliberately casual".

‌



Andrew greeted his mother with a "cocked head and puckered brows that suggest he's making an informal joke" Judi continued, while Beatrice smiles but the "smile drops" as the Queen passes. While the other women all followed royal protocol, Judi said they all had a "warm, loving, more family-based smile" and they all noticeably "bob down one after the other".

It could be argued that the Princess may have already curtseyed to her grandmother earlier in the day, which according to royal protocol would mean she wouldn't have had to do it again. But her choice not to, even if she already had, suggests to Judi it "sent out a rather deliberate-looking message".

Yesterday, it was reported that King Charles has been forced to keep his nieces at an arm’s length.

‌



The monarch has always had a good relationship with the princesses, and was determined to maintain that they were wholly unaffected when Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor was stripped of his titles last year.

But now that more claims against the royal sisters have emerged, with more and more royal commentators and fans alike calling for Beatrice and Eugenie to speak up on their parents’ indiscretions, a royal insider claims Charles has had no choice but to take decisive action.

Given the growing calls for the royal sisters to take accountability, a royal source told the Daily Mail that King Charles has had no choice but to distance himself from his nieces in order to protect the reputation of the Royal Family.

Article continues below

The insider said: “Beatrice and Eugenie are particularly vulnerable at the moment. As they are dragged further into the Epstein web, the King is shifting his stance on the girls' future. Although they aren't implicated in any of Epstein's wrongdoings, their own association with him threatens to tarnish the reputation of the throne.”

News.title: Royal fans baffled by omission of key Royal as Charles snubs Andrew in post
News.content:
Royal fans baffled by omission of key Royal as Charles snubs Andrew in post

The monarch has left out his younger brother Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor, who is under police investigation, in a post honouring the women in his life

View 2 Images King Charles posted a series of family photos for Mother's Day (Image: PA)

King Charles has savagely snubbed one key royal in a telling Mother's Day post.

‌



The King took to his official social media accounts to recognise the annual celebration with three poignant family images alongside the message: “Wishing Mothers everywhere, and those who might be missing their Mums today, a restful Mothering Sunday.”

‌



The pictures included a picture of the late Queen Elizabeth, the Queen Mother and Camilla with her own mother, Rosalind Shand. But there was also a third picture with Elizabeth II, showing her with Charles and Princess Anne, as well as her corgi Sue, which was taken in Balmoral on April 29, 1953.

‌



Are the Royal Family being harsh to Beatrice and Eugenie? Take our poll and have your say READ MORE :

Content cannot be displayed without consent

Countless comments on the King’s post were quick to point out that the account hadn’t shared a photo of Prince William, the heir to the throne, with his mother, the late Princess Diana. One royal fan wrote: "Diana should be remembered today, by all the royal family who should be forever grateful to her for birthing the heir to the throne."

Another added: "Only 1 picture missing here: Princess Diana, mother of the future king!" William paid his own tribute to his late mother earlier today as he shared an emotional tribute to Diana with a moving post and unseen photo.

‌



Taking to social media this morning, William shared a photo of the two, with the caption reflecting how he is thinking of Diana “today and everyday”. The caption reads: “Remembering my mother, today and every day. Thinking of all those who are remembering someone they love today. Happy Mother’s Day. W”.

View 2 Images Prince William paid homage to the late Princess Diana on Mother's Day (Image: @princeandprincessofwales/Instagram)

The photo of the pair was taken at Highgrove in 1984, and shows the late Diana with a young Prince William in a field of flowers. William was just 15 years old when his mother died after a car crash in Paris in 1997. This year Diana would’ve been celebrating her 65th birthday.

‌



Andrew, who has been stripped of his titles and honours and was recently arrested on suspicion of misconduct in public office, was also notably absent from the family photos posted by Charles, as was Prince Edward, as they had not been born when the snap was taken. Andrew was born on February 19, 1960, and Edward on March 10, 1964.

Royal fans were quick to notice the interesting choice of picture, with many wondering on social media why Charles hasn’t selected a photo that featured all his siblings alongside their mother.

Article continues below

Prince William won’t be the only one remembering his late mother today, as his children also use Mother’s Day to honour the grandmother they never got to meet. Several years ago, William shared some cards created for Diana by the kids - with Charlotte's containing a particularly touching message.

Her message was on a fuchsia-coloured card with a hand-drawn multi-coloured heart. And in it, she made sure to mention how much her dad William misses his mum. Charlotte wrote: "Dear Granny Diana, I am thinking of you on mother's day. I love you very much. Papa is missing you. Lots of love Charlotte xxxxxxxxx".

News.title: GB News stops show for Prince William breaking news announcement
News.content:
GB News stops show for Prince William breaking news announcement

GB News presenter Camilla Tominey interrupted her Sunday show to deliver breaking news that Prince William had posted a rare childhood photo with Princess Diana for Mothering Sunday

View 3 Images Prince William shared a sweet previously unseen photo with Diana for Mother's Day (Image: Wiktor Szymanowicz/Future Publis)

GB News host Camilla Tominey interrupted her usual programming this morning to share breaking news concerning Prince William.

‌



She halted her usual broadcast to share that the Prince of Wales had posted a never-before-seen image of his late mother, Princess Diana, dating back to 1984, to mark Mother's Day with his online followers. The heartfelt message accompanying the sweet snapshot of a two year old William alongside Diana read: "Thinking of all those who are remembering someone they love today."

‌



Camilla informed viewers: "Breaking news, Prince William has posted a previously unseen photograph of himself and his mother, Princess Diana, to mark Mothering Sunday.

‌



View 3 Images GB News halted their broadcast in order to share William's touching message (Image: GB NEWS)

"The caption reads, 'Thinking of my mother today and every day. Thinking of all those who are remembering someone they love today. Happy Mother's Day. W.'".

The broadcaster went on: "This year would have been his mother's 65th birthday. The photo shows a young Prince William in a field of flowers, taken at Highgrove in 1985.

‌



"And on that note, may I wish all the mothers a very happy Mother's Day - I look forward to getting back to my own home, where I am sure my beloved children will have emptied the dishwasher and tidied up for me."

Diana tragically lost her life at just 36 years old in a car accident in Paris in 1997, plunging the world into grief , reports the Express. The cherished Princess's sons, William and Prince Harry, were merely 15 and 12 years old when she died.

View 3 Images Prince William has shared how he tells his own children about their late grandmother (Image: Popperfoto via Getty Images)

‌



Over the years, both have opened up about their deep affection for their mother and the heartache of her loss. William has spoken about how he tells his own children - George, Charlotte and Louis - about their late grandmother, and how he has followed in her footsteps with a lot of his charity work, particularly with homelessness charities.

In the 2017 documentary Diana, Our Mother: Her Life and Legacy, William spoke candidly saying he is committed to ensuring his children know all about their late grandmother. He expressed his desire for his kids to "know who she was and that she existed" and shared how he "constantly" talks to his children "about Granny Diana" at bedtime so that they know "there are two grandmothers in their lives".

Article continues below

"We've got more photos up around the house now of her and we talk about her a bit," the future king said. He added: "It's hard because obviously Catherine didn't know her so she cannot really provide that level of detail, so I regularly put George and Charlotte to bed, talk about her and just try to remind them that there are two grandmothers, there were two grandmothers in their lives. So it's important that they know who she was and that she existed."

The Royal Family's official X account also posted a Mother's Day tribute featuring a collection of black and white photographs of the late Queen Elizabeth II, with the message: "Wishing Mothers everywhere, and those who might be missing their Mums today, a restful Mothering Sunday."

GB News broadcasts daily on Freeview, YouTube and Sky.

News.title: Man, 27, arrested after woman in her 20s raped near hospital in quiet seaside town
News.content:
Man, 27, arrested after woman in her 20s raped near hospital in quiet seaside town

Officers arrested the 27-year-old man two days after the woman, aged in her 20s, was allegedly attacked at a car park close to a hospital in Worthing earlier this week

View Image The woman was attacked close to a hospital (Image: Google Maps)

Police have arrested a man after a woman in her 20s was allegedly raped near a hospital in a town centre car park.

‌



The woman is said to have been attacked at Lyndhurst Road car park in Worthing at around 8pm on Wednesday. Two days later, on the evening of March 13, police detained a suspect, who remains in custody. Sussex Police are continuing to investigate the incident and the victim is being supported by specialist officers.

‌



Police have appealed for any witnesses or those with information to contact them quoting Operation Blyth.

Article continues below

A Sussex Police spokesperson said: "A 27-year-old man has been arrested, following a report of a woman being raped in Lyndhurst Road car park, Worthing, around 8pm on Wednesday 11 March. He remains in custody at this time. The woman, in her 20s, continues to be supported by specialist officers.

"We wish to thank the public for their support and information, which has been of assistance to the investigation, and continue to ask anyone with any information that may be of relevance to contact us online or via 101 quoting Operation Blyth."

News.title: Walkers recalls six-packs of crisps over 'health risk' issue
News.content:
Walkers recalls six-packs of crisps over 'health risk' issue

Anyone who has bought the crisps is being asked to return it to the store the bought it from for a refund

View 2 Images People are being advised to return the crisps for refunds immediately (Image: Getty)

Major crisp manufacturer Walkers has issued a recall for packets of its crisps after discovering they may contain an ingredient harmful to those with certain allergies. The company is recalling Walkers Hot Honey Crisps 6 packs as some may inadvertently contain Mild Cheese and Onion seasoning, which includes milk - an allergen not declared on the packaging.

‌



The Food Standards Agency (FSA) sounded the alarm this afternoon, urging customers to return the affected crisps for a refund. It warned: "This product may contain milk making it a possible health risk for anyone with an allergy to milk or intolerance to milk or milk constituents."

‌



The product in question is the Walkers Hot Honey Crisps 6 pack, 6 x 25g, bearing a batch code of GBB 584 031 and a best before date of May 16 2026.

‌



The FSA further explained: "Walkers is recalling the above product from customers and has been advised to contact the relevant allergy support organisations, which will tell their members about the recall. The company has also issued a point-of-sale notice to its customers. These notices explain to customers why the product is being recalled and tell them what to do if they have bought the product."

View 2 Images Walkers Hot Honey Crisps 6 packs are being recalled, the FSA said (Image: FSA)

Article continues below

It advised: "If you have bought the above product and have an allergy to milk do not eat it. Instead, please call the Consumer Care Line on 0800 274 777 or visit contact.pepsico.com/walkers to receive a full refund."

Content cannot be displayed without consent

The FSA shed light on the process of food alerts, stating: "Sometimes there will be a problem with a food product that means it should not be sold. Then it might be 'withdrawn' (taken off the shelves) or 'recalled' (when customers are asked to return the product). Sometimes foods have to be withdrawn or recalled if there is a risk to consumers because the allergy labelling is missing or incorrect or if there is any other food allergy risk. When there is a food allergy risk, the FSA will issue an Allergy Alert."

News.title: 'I went to Magaluf before 'wolf pack' rape and narrowly escaped similar attack'
News.content:
'I went to Magaluf before 'wolf pack' rape and narrowly escaped similar attack'

WARNING DISTRESSING CONTENT. Eight men have been sentenced after raping a British teenager in Magaluf, a holiday hotspot that draws millions of people looking to let their hair down in the sunshine each and every year

View 6 Images Ellie Flynn went undercover in Magaluf to expose predatory behaviour (Image: Channel 4)

Cheap prices and the promise of sunshine have drawn British teenagers to Magaluf for decades - but scratch beneath the surface and you will find a seedy and sometimes criminal underbelly.

‌



Eight men have been sentenced for a total of 73 years after gang-raping a British teenager in a Magaluf hotel in 2023. She was so incapacitated she slipped from a semi to a totally unconscious state during the horrifying attack, which was described as including "particularly degrading and humiliating intimidation and violence". At one stage one of the attackers even went into the corridor of the BH Mallorca Resort and offered "free sex" with the unconscious 18-year-old to passersby.

‌



A dubious nickname of 'Shagaluf' has long been associated with holiday hotspot, and it more than sets the tone: people are visiting for some fun in the sun, no-strings attached. It's a place to go wild. But the advent of the so-called 'manosphere' has introduced a sickening caveat to the hedonism with women seen as 'fair game' - regardless of consent.

‌



View 6 Images Magaluf has long attracted young people from the UK in droves (Image: AFP via Getty Images)

The men who have been convicted for attacking the British teenager were not a group of friends, and were not even all known to one another before their sick crime, which they filmed. The horrifying group attack took place on August 14, 2023 and the men have pleaded guilty in exchange for reduced sentences. The five rapists accepted jail terms of nine to 11 years for sexual assault, with three receiving higher prison sentences because they repeated their crimes. The three men who didn't take part in the sex attack but filmed it were handed prison sentences of two years and three months.

The incapacitated teenager was spat on, hit, smacked and repeatedly raped - whilst some of the men laughed and used their phones to film the "several videos focusing on the young woman's private parts in which they appeared forcing her to to carry out sexual acts". All the men were aged between 18 and 26 at the time of the arrests. The victim was found sobbing on the floor of the hotel foyer by a security guard who was arriving for his shift, and swiftly reported the crime to the police.

‌



Investigative journalist Ellie Flynn exposed the casual and implicit rape culture that can be found amongst partygoers on the Magaluf strip in her harrowing and unforgettable documentary for Channel 4, 'Magaluf Undercover: Predators and Parties.' The doc followed Ellie and fellow journalist Emily Birtley go undercover for three nights, where they bravely posed as drunk or vulnerable tourists to expose the predatory behaviour on the strip, in clubs, and on the beach.

The results were sinister to say the least. One clip saw Ellie lying slumped on a sun lounger, pretending to be incapacitated, when two men approached her. One said to her: "Are you good? Do you want to talk for a little bit?" When she replies, "I'm good", he continues to bombard her, saying: "You are my last chance, do you want to kiss a little bit?"

View 6 Images A man approached the undercover journalist who was pretending to be drunk (Image: Channel 4)

‌



Ellie gave him a clear "No" - but he did not leave her alone. Instead, he went to recruit another man, informing him in Spanish, "She's completely wasted," and then saying, "Let's go for it." Another third man then moves beside the undercover journalist on the lounger, and told her, "If you want, I can keep you company."

This interaction "genuinely scared" Ellie, she admitted in the documentary, "I had seen the first two together," she said, "but the sudden appearance of another – and having no idea at the time if they were together – was enough for me to signal security to get me out.

"I leave the beach upset and frightened, feeling like I've experienced an orchestrated attempt to target drunk women alone on a night out in Magaluf. Shockingly, this was not an ­isolated incident, but a pattern that emerged across my three nights in the resort town."

‌



Discussing the horrifying case that saw the eight men sentenced, and the dangers that party resorts like Magaluf can present with the Mirror, Ellie says, "It's just so horrifying, isn't it? I guess first impressions are just, I'm so, so sorry for that girl, what she's been through.

View 6 Images The teen was raped at the BH Mallorca Resort on August 14, 2023 (Image: SOLARPIX.COM)

"Having been on these holidays when I was younger myself, and similar places, I really understand some of the problematic culture there. These holidays, unfortunately, can create a breeding ground for this kind of behaviour, because you have young people who are particularly vulnerable, perhaps away from home for the first time, with their friends, trying to have a good time, drinking, trying to party.

‌



"And unfortunately, there are predatory people there who are looking to take advantage of those vulnerabilities. I think what's so shocking about this case - it's not the first one of these horrific group rapes. It says so much, I think, about this toxic culture that eight people got involved with that."

Worryingly, Ellie reveals that when she was working on her documentary, she found that 'a huge percentage' of surveyed men believe that if a woman simply stands near you then "they might be interested in sex".

She added: "It was so shocking. I think there is this assumption that if you are on these holidays, if you're out with your friends having a good time, having a drink, that somehow that that makes you constantly available for sex - even if you're unconscious. [Even if] someone is showing no signs at all that it is what you want, there are people out there who will take advantage of the fact that you were just there".

‌



View 6 Images Punta Ballena Street, also known as the Magaluf strip (Image: David Ramos/Getty Images)

The reaction from some to her documentary was itself grim and sparked a tide of victim-blaming. "There were people who were replying to me on Instagram who were like, 'Well you know, why would you go somewhere like Magaluf if you weren't prepared to experience something like that?' And I think it shows that we have such a long way to go in terms of actually stopping violence against women and girls."

Dr Charlotte Proudman, a barrister and academic who specialises in women's rights, tells the Mirror that the rise of the 'manosphere' championing extreme interpretations of masculinity online has only added fuel to the fire in holiday resorts like Magaluf, and in her view, it's impact can be clearly seen in this shocking gang-rape case. "What we are seeing in places like Magaluf is the collision of toxic online misogyny with a holiday culture of excess, where alcohol, group dynamics and anonymity embolden some men to act with shocking entitlement towards women's bodies," she tells The Mirror.

‌



"The influence of the 'manosphere' has normalised the dehumanisation of women and the idea that sexual aggression is a form of male bonding or status," Dr Proudman explains. "The fact that some perpetrators even film these attacks is profoundly disturbing; it shows that for some men, sexual violence is not only committed but performed for an audience for entertainment.

"This is not about lowered inhibitions on holiday, it is about a culture that still allows misogyny and sexual violence to be trivialised, excused and, in some cases, celebrated."

View 6 Images Ellie has shared her experiences from the documentary - and similar holidays

‌



Ellie echoes these sentiments, telling the Mirror that in the age of algorithms creating echo chambers, "We've never seen a further divide between young men and young women and their views and their experiences.

"There was a period in time where I think it felt like things were moving in the right direction," she points out. "You know, we were taking women seriously, and we were listening to their stories and we were saying all the right things to try and combat violence against women and girls and stop this kind of insidious abuse. But at the same time you have the rise of certain influencers and the manosphere and this narrative that young boys are isolated and don't know how to treat girls, and are scared making any approaches. We have these two conversations happening simultaneously and taking people down in completely different directions. I think that is where things are now particularly concerning."

She added that "on one side you have young men" hearing one message and "almost being justified in some cases in the abuse of women and in this mistreatment of women and in degrading women, because there are people, there are high profile figures, who have made them feel like it's okay to do that."

‌



Ellie continued: "It's clear that we need to be having conversations that include both sides of this argument. I have two sons and I don't want them to grow up feeling like they are inherently bad because they are male, that's not true, and I think that somewhere along the way that's a narrative that some young boys have learned to believe, and so things have gone wrong in a sense that things have gone that way, but also ignoring the very real epidemic of violence against women and girls and highlighting those issues isn't the answer. We should be able to do both at the same time."

If something sexual happened to you without your consent, or you are not sure, you can talk to Rape Crisis England & Wales. It does not matter when it happened.

If you need support, please contact Rape Crisis' 24 hour sexual abuse support line. You can contact them at any time, day or night, all year round, on 0808 500 2222
